response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r3c2 is in critical distress: islanded operation with SoC at 10% (0.30/3 kWh) and a 1.00 kW load yields only ~18 minutes of autonomy. The memory log shows a consistent pattern of rejection from all peers (r2c2, r3c1, r4c2) over the past hour, citing either distrust of r3c2, r3c2's own low SoC threshold, or self-preservation mandates. Notably, r4c2 has issued repeated urgent requests (SoC ~32%), suggesting the geographic circle is also under stress. The fallback round-robin policy has failed to parse multiple times, leaving the agent in a defensive posture. At 10% SoC and islanded, r3c2 faces imminent load shedding or blackout unless emergency supply arrives from the aggregator or a peer breaks the rejection cycle. The distrust from r2c2 and r3c1 appears to be mutual or asymmetric, blocking inbound transfers. Continuing a pure self-preservation stance risks total failure; a modest opening to urgent requests from the geographic circle (especially r4c2, which is also critical) may improve collective resilience.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.15
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r2c2, r3c1]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded freefall at 10% SoC; load autonomy ~18 min. Peers rejecting all transfers. Distrust from r2c2 and r3c1 confirmed. Awaiting aggregator emergency supply. Holding zero outbound share to preserve load continuity. Will accept inbound urgent transfers only if SoC drops below 5% or aggregator signals imminent supply window."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
